'''Nick Bradley-Qalilawa''' (born 28 March 1980) is a former [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s.

==Background==
Bradley-Qalilawa was born in [[Darlinghurst, New South Wales]], Australia.

==Career in Australia==
Bradley-Qalilawa is a [[North Sydney Bears]] junior playing every grade in the district except first-grade and won a [[Jersey Flegg Cup|Jersey Flegg]] Grand final in 1998. He played at the [[Wests Tigers]], making his first grade début in 2001, [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly]] and [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]]/[[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]]. He primarily played on the wing.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.weststigers.com.au/about/first-grade-players/|title=WESTS TIGERS FIRST GRADE PLAYERS|website=Wests Tigers}}</ref>

==Career in the Super League==
{{Expand section|date=March 2010}}
Bradley-Qalilawa joined the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]] in 2005 and played for the club under its new guise as [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]] in 2006. He left the club at the conclusion of 2006's [[Super League XI]], scoring 26 tries in 55 games.

==Representative career==
He was named in the [[Fiji national rugby league team#2008 World Cup Training Squad|Fiji]] squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Samoa, Tonga and Fiji name squads">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7658506.stm|title=Samoa, Tonga and Fiji name squads|publisher=BBC|date=8 October 2008|access-date=9 October 2008}}</ref>
